patient_data_confirmation: Presenting complaint: Right carpus swelling and limping after agility training

history_review.owner_concern: Dog still limping but less painful

risk_factor_assessment: Contact with other animals (possible minor trauma)

diagnostic_plan.primary_imaging: Radiographs of right carpus to rule out fractures or bone lesions
additional_tests_if_needed: CBC and serum chemistry if systemic signs develop
Joint fluid analysis only if swelling worsens or persists >7 days

initial_management.NSAIDs: Continue meloxicam if tolerated, 0.1 mg/kg PO q24h
initial_management.rest_and_activity_restriction: Strict rest; avoid agility training for 10–14 days
supportive_care: Apply a supportive bandage if pain during ambulation

owner_education_and_monitoring.recheck_schedule: 3–5 days; if no improvement, consider advanced imaging (MRI) or referral
